articles.title: AUTOMATICALLY.
articles.summary: Most teams only discover broken flows after metrics tank. FlowAI Insights [4] by Userflow [5] changes that.

articles.title: THE EXTRAORDINARY AS MUNDANE
articles.summary: AI has made extraordinary capabilities feel mundane while quietly shifting the bottleneck from execution to judgment. The real risk is not job loss in tech, but how few people have access to tools that could fundamentally change how they work.

articles.title: THE FEATURE BACKLOG HAS GONE *POOF*
articles.summary: AI coding agents are collapsing feature backlogs and redefining software velocity. The winners will be teams that pair agentic engineering with strong human judgment about what to build next.

articles.title: THE CIRCULAR LOGIC OF OUR METRICS
articles.summary: Product teams often mistake what is loud and measurable for what is valuable. Real strategy requires resisting dashboard-driven noise and defining success on your own terms.

articles.title: THE HIDDEN DANGER OF SHIPPING FAST
articles.summary: When shipping outpaces adoption, velocity turns into waste. The winners will be teams that stay fast while being ruthless about what deserves attention.

articles.title: MOZILLA PIONEERS IS ACCEPTING APPLICATIONS UNTIL FEBRUARY 16TH!
articles.summary: This is a paid 2 month product residency, during which you will partner with members of Mozilla's New Products team to explore, identify, validate and build product concepts. If you want to help build the web and AI tools that you want to see in the world - Apply

articles.title: AGENTS VS. WORKFLOWS: THE FRAMEWORK FOUNDERS ACTUALLY NEED
articles.summary: The Replit failure highlights why autonomous AI agents are risky in production. For most teams, workflows with constrained LLM components are safer, faster to ship, and easier to trust.

articles.title: SCRIPT FOR ORCHESTRATING MULTI-STEP AI PROJECT IMPLEMENTATION USING PLANS AND PRDS
articles.summary: A bash orchestrator that runs a numbered plan sequence through an AI coding harness, with logging, stateful resume, validation checks, and optional git automation. It can also run a final review that generates audit notes and structured fix plans.

articles.title: POST MORTEM THEATRE
articles.summary: Post-mortems fail when they focus on behavior instead of systems. Real learning happens when concrete system changes make the same failure harder to repeat.

articles.title: HOPE, CONTEXT, AND CONTROL
articles.summary: AI sharpens the conflict between control and collective sensemaking. It can expand shared context and creativity, or it can accelerate centralized, technocratic control. The outcome is still undecided.

articles.title: ENABLE YOUR AGENTIC AI PRODUCT WITH THIRD-PARTY TOOLS
articles.summary: This tutorial covers everything you need to build an AI agent SaaS feature that takes action in users' external apps.

articles.title: CONFUSING ARTIFACT QUALITY WITH CERTAINTY
articles.summary: AI boosts the appearance of productivity, but polished outputs often mask untested assumptions.

articles.title: WHERE MOBILE MONEY GOES NOW
articles.summary: In 2025, apps overtook games in mobile revenue as subscriptions and generative AI accelerated growth.
